articles.title: Strait of Hormuz Closure Pushes Crude Prices Higher

Crude oil and gasoline prices settled sharply higher on Monday after President Trump rejected Iran's response to his latest peace proposal, which prolongs the closure of the Strait...

articles.title: FX Watch: EUR/USD and USD/JPY Setups to Watch if U.S. CPI Comes in Hot
articles.expand: If Uncle Sam drops a much hotter inflation picture, USD traders may want to check EUR/USD and USD/JPY charts for opportunities.

articles.title: Gold eases from three-week top as Iran tensions and hawkish Fed bets support USD
articles.expand: Gold (XAU/USD) retreats from a three-week high, touched during the Asian session on Tuesday, as traders await the release of the latest US consumer inflation figures before positioning for the next leg of a directional move.

articles.title: Alphabet plans first yen bond sale to fund $190 billion AI spending push
articles.expand: Alphabet is planning its first yen-denominated bond sale across up to eight maturities, expected to total several hundred billion yen, as part of a broader push to fund $190 billion in AI capital spending this year. Summary: Alphabet is planning its first yen-denominated bond sale in a multi-tranche offering spanning maturities of three, five, seve...

articles.title: Recap - Japan and US reaffirm currency cooperation after Bessent's Tokyo talks
articles.expand: Japan and the US reaffirmed close coordination on currency markets, including intervention, after Finance Minister Katayama met Bessent in Tokyo, as Japan is suspected to have spent $63.5 billion defending the yen. Summary: Japanese Finance Minister Katayama said Japan and the US reaffirmed close cooperation on exchange rate moves, including curren...

articles.title: BoJ Summary Shows Growing Support for Near-Term Rate Hike
articles.expand: Bank of Japan’s Summary of Opinions from the April meeting revealed a noticeably more hawkish debate inside the board, with several policymakers arguing that another interest rate hike could come sooner rather than later as the Iran war intensifies inflation pressures. One member explicitly said “it is quite possible the BoJ will raise interest rat...

articles.title: WTI drifts higher above $95.50 on US-Iran tensions, Strait of Hormuz disruption fears
articles.expand: West Texas Intermediate (WTI), the US crude oil benchmark, is trading around $95.70 during the early Asian trading hours on Tuesday. The WTI price edges higher amid renewed geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

articles.title: FX Watch: USD/CHF and AUD/USD Triangle Breakouts If U.S. CPI Fails to Impress
articles.expand: Markets are expecting a significantly upbeat U.S. CPI to tip the scales in favor of Fed tightening soon, but what if the numbers fail to impress? Here’s what I’m watching on USD/CHF and AUD/USD in this scenario.

articles.title: Australian business confidence remains deeply pessimistic, -24 in April (prior -29)
articles.expand: Australian business confidence edged up to -24 in April from -29 in March but remained deeply negative, as surging energy costs squeezed margins and conditions fell to their lowest since 2020, NAB data showed. Summary: NAB's business confidence index improved marginally to -24 in April from -29 in March, remaining deeply negative after March's seco...

articles.title: AUD/USD Price Forecast: Eyes nine-day EMA support near 0.7200
articles.expand: AUD/USD loses ground after two days of gains, trading around 0.7240 during the Asian hours on Tuesday. The technical analysis of the daily chart indicates that the pair is moving upwards within the ascending channel, suggesting an ongoing bullish bias.

articles.title: Japanese Yen drifts lower vs USD as weak Household Spending data counters hawkish BoJ
articles.expand: The USD/JPY pair attracts some buyers for the second straight day and advances to a four-day high following the disappointing release of Japan's Household Spending data this Tuesday.

articles.title: Asia open: Stocks hit new highs on AI optimism as US-Iran ceasefire hangs by a thread
articles.expand: Global stocks surged to new record highs as AI optimism continued to dominate market sentiment despite escalating concerns over the fragile US-Iran ceasefire. articles.title: Euro softens below 1.1800 on US-Iran ceasefire risks, traders await US CPI data
articles.expand: The EUR/USD pair loses ground to near 1.1775 during the early Asian session on Tuesday. The Euro (EUR) softens against the US Dollar (USD) as traders turn cautious ahead of the US April inflation report and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Summary: Bessent arrived in Tokyo on Monday for meetings with Finance Minister Katayama and Prime Minister Takaichi on Tuesday, his third visi...

articles.title: BOJ 'Summary" - Japan rate hike back on table as BOJ signals next move still likely upward
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan held rates at its April meeting but warned upside inflation risks are rising as crude oil prices surge, with several members flagging possible hikes as soon as the next meeting. Summary: The Bank of Japan kept its policy interest rate unchanged at its April 27-28 meeting, citing the need for a wait-and-see approach given the uncer...

articles.title: New Fed Chair Warsh could be locked in as soon as Tuesday
articles.expand: Kevin Warsh, Trump's nominee for Federal Reserve chair, cleared a key Senate procedural vote on Monday, moving him toward confirmation as early as Tuesday for a 14-year term as Fed governor. A separate vote on his four-year term as Fed chair is expected as soon as Wednesday, ahead of Jerome Powell's departure on Friday. This article was written by ...

articles.title: UK consumer spending falls for first time since 2024 as Iran war bites
articles.expand: UK consumer spending fell 0.1% in April, the first annual decline since late 2024, as Iran war fears drove households toward savings and away from big-ticket and travel spending, Barclays and BRC data showed. Summary: Barclays credit and debit card spending fell 0.1% year-on-year in April, reversing a 0.9% gain in March, the first decline since Nov...

articles.title: Gold drifts higher to near $4,750 ahead of US CPI inflation release
articles.expand: Gold price (XAU/USD) trades in positive territory around $4,750 during the early Asian session on Tuesday. The precious metal edges higher as traders assess developments in the United States (US)-Iran diplomacy and await key US inflation data, which is due later on Tuesday.

articles.title: Pound Sterling slips from peak as US CPI and UK GDP loom
articles.expand: Sterling pulled back from a fresh peak near 1.3650 on Monday, easing close to 1.3610 through European trade after the Asian session squeezed the Pound to a new local high.

articles.title: China: Reflation momentum delays PBoC cuts – ING
articles.expand: ING’s Lynn Song notes that stronger China Consumer Price Index (CPI) and Producer Price Index (PPI) data in April, alongside resilient exports, reinforce a reflation narrative that reduces urgency for People’s Bank of China (PBoC) easing.

articles.title: Economic and event calendar Asia Tuesday, May 12, 2026 - BoJ will give us their thoughts
articles.expand: The Bank of Japan will release its 'Summary' of the April meeting today. This was an 'on hold' meeting, but was eventful. From the day: The Bank of Japan left its short-term policy rate unchanged at 0.75% , as widely expected, but delivered a significantly more hawkish inflation outlook alongside the decision, sharply revising up its price forecast...

articles.title: AUDUSD finds support near 100 hour MA. Staying above keeps the buyers in control
articles.expand: The AUDUSD initially dipped during the Asia-Pacific session, but buyers stepped in against a key swing area between 0.7221 and 0.7227. That zone had acted as a ceiling from mid-April through early May before the pair finally broke higher. Since then, the price has traded back and forth around the area, with the level alternating between support and...

articles.title: Japan: Wage gains, confidence risks and BoJ – Rabobank
articles.expand: Rabobank’s Senior FX Strategist Jane Foley notes Japanese wage data have improved, with unions securing solid ‘shunto’ wage hikes and real wages rising again, supporting the Bank of Japan’s (BoJ) desired virtuous cycle.

articles.title: Australian Dollar Price Forecast: Extra gains still on the cards
articles.expand: The Aussie Dollar continues its march north almost unabated, lifting AUD/USD to the area of multi-year tops. For now, the pair’s positive outlook remains unchanged, propped up by elevated inflation in Oz and the RBA’s hawkish stance.

articles.title: BNM may cut rates if growth disappoints amid uncertainties
articles.expand: PETALING JAYA: Demand destruction in Malaysia's trading partners will begin to outweigh the benefits of higher energy prices enjoyed by the country, according to BMI.

articles.title: Whittaker’s ‘extremely disappointed’ at ethical ranking drop
articles.expand: The chocolate-maker said its ranking outside the Chocolate Scorecard top 10 was a surprise, and not the result of ethical standards slipping at the company.

articles.title: US energy secretary pleased NZ committing to fossil fuels
articles.expand: Trip report reveals Chris Wright, whose country dominates LNG exports, was happy NZ had planned an import terminal.

articles.title: China's April inflation data beats across board as energy costs reshape price landscape
articles.expand: China's April PPI jumped 2.8% year-on-year, a 45-month high and well above forecasts, while CPI rose 1.2%, as Iran war energy costs end a 41-month deflationary streak. articles.expand: As the prospects of a peace deal between the USA and Iran seemed to rise again, risk assets and stocks rose last week, while energy prices took a hit. Find out if a turnaround might happen this week as we face another Trump war deadline due Thursday.
